machinery_genes.role: lytic transglycosylase (peptidoglycan remodelling, facilitates apparatus assembly)
machinery_genes.quote: The Brucella abortus virB operon, consisting of 11 genes, virB1 to virB11, and two putative genes, orf12 (virB12) and orf13, encodes a type IV secretion system (T4SS)
machinery_genes.note: Named directly on B. abortus 2308 (Sun et al 2005). Transglycosylase role from VirB1 family vocabulary in the apparatus brief; the Xiong 2021 review notes the VirB1 N-terminus is a lytic transglycosylase.

machinery_genes.quote: the stretching needle complex (composed of VirB2)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021 review of the Brucella VirB system; VirB2 named as the pilus/needle subunit. Also covered by the operon-wide naming quote 'an operon encoded by VirB1-VirB12'.

machinery_genes.quote: the inner membrane complex (composed of VirB3, VirB4, VirB6, VirB8, and the N-terminus of VirB10)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021 places VirB3 in the Brucella VirB inner-membrane complex. Named in the operon list 'an operon encoded by VirB1-VirB12'.

machinery_genes.quote: the ATPases/energy center (consisting of VirB4 and VirB11)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021; VirB4 named as one of the two Brucella VirB ATPases.

machinery_genes.quote: the linking stalk (probably composed of fragments from VirB5 or VirB10)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021. VirB5 is also one of the genes whose mutants O'Callaghan 1999 showed attenuate the sister-strain B. suis 1330 VirB system. Named in 'VirB1-VirB12'.

machinery_genes.quote: the inner membrane complex (composed of VirB3, VirB4, VirB6, VirB8, and the N-terminus of VirB10)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021 places VirB6 in the Brucella VirB inner-membrane complex.

machinery_genes.quote: the core/outer membrane complex (composed of VirB7, VirB9, and VirB10)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021; VirB7 named in the Brucella VirB outer-membrane core complex. Also in the operon list virB1 to virB11 (Sun 2005).

machinery_genes.quote: the inner membrane complex (composed of VirB3, VirB4, VirB6, VirB8, and the N-terminus of VirB10)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021 places VirB8 in the Brucella VirB inner-membrane complex. VirB8-specific antisera have been raised against the Brucella protein (Rolan/den Hartigh literature).

machinery_genes.quote: the core/outer membrane complex (composed of VirB7, VirB9, and VirB10)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021; VirB9 named in the Brucella VirB core complex. O'Callaghan 1999 showed virB9 mutants are highly attenuated in the sister-strain B. suis 1330 system.

machinery_genes.quote: VirB10 is a member of this transport apparatus that has homologues in all type IV secretions systems described so far
machinery_genes.note: Sieira et al 2000, the founding apparatus paper on B. abortus 2308; virB10 mutants characterized directly on strain 2308. Bridging role from VirB10 family vocabulary in the apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: the ATPases/energy center (consisting of VirB4 and VirB11)
machinery_genes.note: Xiong 2021; VirB11 named as the second Brucella VirB ATPase. Last gene of the virB1 to virB11 series named on strain 2308 by Sun 2005.

machinery_genes.role: putative outer-membrane lipoprotein (operon terminal gene; non-essential accessory)
machinery_genes.quote: two putative genes, orf12 (virB12) and orf13, encodes a type IV secretion system (T4SS)
machinery_genes.note: orf12/virB12 is the twelfth, lipoprotein-like component named on B. abortus 2308 (corresponds to the 'twelfth ORF encoding a putative lipoprotein' of O'Callaghan 1999 in B. suis). Sun et al 2005 show VirB12 is expressed during infection but is dispensable for the apparatus's essential function; included as a named operon component but flagged as a non-essential accessory. orf13 (the 13th ORF) is excluded: it is a putative gene not assigned a VirB number.

notes: Brucella abortus 2308 VirB is a type-IVA (VirB/D4 archetype) T4SS. The founding apparatus paper on this exact strain is Sieira et al 2000 (J Bacteriol), which cloned/sequenced the virB locus as a collinear arrangement of 13 ORFs forming an operon transcribed from virB1; the sister-strain founding paper is O'Callaghan et al 1999 (B. suis 1330), which named 12 ORFs encoding the 11 Agrobacterium VirB homologues plus a twelfth lipoprotein ORF. Sun et al 2005 (Infect Immun, B. abortus 2308) gives the cleanest per-strain naming: 'virB1 to virB11, and two putative genes, orf12 (virB12) and orf13'. Core apparatus fully covered gene-by-gene: VirB1-VirB11 plus VirB12. KEY DIFFERENCE FROM ARCHETYPE: the Brucella virB operon has NO VirD4 coupling protein (the operon is virB1-virB12 only, on chromosome II); VirD4 is therefore deliberately not listed, in contrast to the canonical Agrobacterium VirB/D4. VirB12 (orf12) is included as a named terminal operon component but is a non-essential accessory lipoprotein (Sun et al 2005 show deletion mutants persist at wild-type levels). orf13, the 13th ORF in Sieira's count, is excluded: it is an unannotated putative gene with no VirB designation. Per-subunit structural roles (needle VirB2; core/OM VirB7/9/10; IM channel VirB3/4/6/8; ATPases VirB4/VirB11; stalk VirB5) are taken verbatim from the Xiong 2021 and Ke 2015 Brucella VirB reviews. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the translocated effectors (this instance's effectors BAB2_0123, BAB2_0466, BAB2_0541, plus literature effectors VceA/VceC/BspA-F etc.), and the VjbR/BvrR-BvrS regulators that control virB transcription. All quotes are verbatim from the cited PubMed/PMC abstracts or full texts.
